calendar_tier: TIER 1 — March Madness Championship weekend. Women's title game completed (UCLA upset), men's title game Monday. This dominates the issue.

top_topics.topic: UCLA women's championship upset over South Carolina

tweet_volume_check.dominant_topic: Championship weekend — women's upset completed, men's title Monday. Double championship coverage leads.

editorial_reasoning.lead_choice: UCLA women's championship upset — 79-51 demolition of South Carolina, program's first NCAA title, third-largest championship margin ever, Big Ten breakthrough. Tier 1 event with massive upset angle.

stories_considered_and_demoted.reason: Tier 3 regular season on Tier 1 championship weekend. Demoted to Around the League.
Off-season content during championship weekend. Skip entirely.

stories_promoted.reason: Monday's title game setup. Big Ten drought, UConn dynasty, perfect second lead.
MVP candidates face-off with great meme content. Strong supporting story.

editorial_reasoning.tweet_gaps: Good UCLA celebration content available. Strong Michigan-UConn preview material. Jokic content solid.

stories.headline: UCLA Runs South Carolina Off The Court For First Championship
hooks: Big Ten's first women's championship since 1999
South Carolina dynasty denied fourth title

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: 79-51 is third-largest margin in championship history. UCLA outrebounded 49-37, held SC to 29% shooting. 25-9 third quarter was largest single-quarter margin in title game history.
sub_angles.running_joke: Dawn Staley's 'disruption' defense got disrupted. South Carolina shot 13% from three.
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Big Ten breakthrough after 27-year drought. Cori Close's 15-year journey. UCLA seniors' legacy.
sub_angles.cross_connection: Big Ten on verge of double championship — Michigan plays Monday.
sub_angles.where_it_goes: UCLA dynasty potential. South Carolina dynasty interrupted. Big Ten conference relevance.

research.context: UCLA's first NCAA title, first Big Ten women's champion since 1999 Purdue
research.connections: Big Ten could sweep both titles if Michigan beats UConn Monday

blocks.hint: Hook — UCLA didn't just beat South Carolina. They ran them off the court, 79-51, in the third-largest championship margin in history. Frame as a demolition, not a game.

blocks.why: Championship announcement tweet with all caps energy

blocks.hint: Context — This was UCLA's first NCAA title. Their first championship game appearance since the tournament began in 1982. 48 years since their AIAW title. Make the drought matter.

blocks.why: Gabriela Jaquez mic drop moment — 'We decided to be National Champions'

blocks.avoid: Don't search basketball — search 'complete destruction' or 'demolition'

blocks.hint: Statistical absurdity — 25-9 third quarter was the largest single-quarter margin in championship history. UCLA outrebounded them 49-37. South Carolina shot 13% from three. The numbers tell the story of complete domination.

blocks.why: Jaquez family celebration photo — perfect emotional beat

blocks.avoid: Don't search generic celebration — search 'vindication' or 'finally'

blocks.hint: Kicker — The Big Ten hasn't won a women's basketball title since 1999. UCLA, in just their second season in the conference, broke a 27-year drought. And Michigan plays for the men's title Monday. The Big Ten is having a moment.

stories.headline: Michigan-UConn Set For Title Game After Saturday Beatdowns
hooks: Big Ten drought vs UConn dynasty

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Michigan's 18-point margin over Arizona. UConn's 19 straight tournament wins beyond second round.
sub_angles.running_joke: Michigan was supposed to struggle. Instead they boat-raced a 1-seed.
sub_angles.who_else_affected: Big Ten trying to end 26-year men's title drought. UConn going for third in four years.
sub_angles.cross_connection: Big Ten could sweep both championships this weekend.
sub_angles.where_it_goes: Legacy implications for both programs and conferences.

research.context: Championship Monday at 8:50 PM ET on TBS
research.connections: Big Ten conference having historic weekend

blocks.hint: Hook — Michigan was supposed to struggle with Arizona. Instead they boat-raced them 91-73. What was billed as the game of the Final Four became a blowout by halftime.

blocks.why: Michigan's 90+ points in five straight games stat

blocks.hint: UConn side — The Huskies did what they do, grinding out Illinois 71-62. This is their third title game in four years. They don't lose these games.

blocks.why: Yaxel Lendeborg injury update for Monday

blocks.avoid: Don't search basketball — search 'epic showdown' or 'final boss'

blocks.hint: Kicker — The Big Ten hasn't won a men's title since Michigan State in 2000. Michigan could end a 26-year drought. Or UConn extends their tournament dominance. Monday night in Indy.

stories.headline: Jokic Shows Wemby Who's Still The King

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Nuggets' eight-game win streak. Jokic's mentorship moment after the game.
sub_angles.running_joke: Jokic's feet were moving faster than ever against Wemby.
sub_angles.who_else_affected: MVP race implications. Nuggets vs Lakers seeding.

blocks.hint: Hook — Every time Wemby gets hype, Jokic reminds everyone who the real MVP is. The Nuggets beat the Spurs in OT and Jokic was on a mission.

blocks.why: Jokic feet moving faster against Wemby meme
Jokic mentoring Wemby after the game

blocks.avoid: Don't search basketball — search 'passing the torch' or 'teaching moment'

blocks.hint: Kicker — The Nuggets are 8-0 in their last eight and Jokic is playing like he's not giving up that MVP crown without a fight.

sub_angles.who_else_affected: UConn players dealing with crazy coach

research.context: Hurley's sideline antics in Final Four

blocks.hint: Hook — Dan Hurley got booed during his postgame interview and had no idea why. This man is genuinely unhinged and it's working.

blocks.why: Hurley talking to himself after win

blocks.hint: Kicker — UConn has won 19 straight tournament games past the second round. Hurley's methods are insane but they work.

stories.headline: Lakers Season Goes From Bad To Worse

sub_angles.statistically_interesting: Lakers lead for 3-seed down to half game

blocks.hint: Hook — Austin Reaves is out 4-6 weeks with an oblique injury. The Lakers' playoff seeding is collapsing and LeBron is back to carrying scrubs.

blocks.why: Lakers without Luka and Reaves, LeBron doing it all

blocks.hint: Kicker — Their lead for the 3-seed is down to half a game. The Nuggets are right behind them. This could get ugly fast.
Hook — Stephon Gilmore retired after 14 seasons. Two-time All-Pro, 2019 Defensive Player of the Year. A cornerback who anchored the Patriots' last championship run.
Kicker — Canton is calling. Gilmore was the best corner of his era when he was locked in.
